name: Via Augusta and the Millenary Olive Trees Loop
name_original: Los Olivos milenarios de Canet lo Roig — Circular por la Vía Augusta
description: Cycle the historic Via Augusta and marvel at ancient millenary olive trees on this moderate 18.3-mile (29.4 km) touring cycling loop in Spai
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Traiguera traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills, ancient olive groves, and distant mountain views, occasionally offering glimpses of the Mediterranean Sea. The region, situated in the province of Castellón, features a dedicated network of cycling routes, including the IMBA-approved "RED BTT 100K of Traiguera." This area provides diverse terrain, from quiet rural lanes winding through agricultural fields to more demanding trails with technical sections. Proximity to the Sierra de Irta Natural Park further enhances the cycling experience with its mountainous landscapes and unspoiled coastline. Best touring cycling routes around Traiguera The most popular touring cycling route is Via Augusta and the Millenary Olive Trees Loop, a 18.3 miles (29.4 km) trail that takes about 2 hours 5 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores historical paths and scenic ancient olive groves. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is San Mateu – Santa Magdalena de Pulpis loop from Traiguera, a difficult 52.5 miles (84.5 km) path. This route offers a longer, more challenging ride through the wider regional landscape. Local touring cyclists also love the View of Vallibona – Vallibona loop from Traiguera, a 57.3 miles (92.2 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 6 hours 23 minutes. Touring cycling around Traiguera is defined by mountain and Mediterranean views, ancient olive groves, and the diverse terrain of the Sierra de Irta Natural Park. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Traiguera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 900 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Traiguera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Traiguera?
A: Traiguera offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 120 different options available for exploration. These routes cater to various preferences, from leisurely rides to more challenging expeditions.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while cycling in Traiguera?
A: While touring cycling in Traiguera, you'll experience diverse landscapes including rolling hills, extensive ancient olive groves, and distant mountain vistas. Some routes even offer glimpses of the Mediterranean Sea. The region also features quiet rural lanes and agricultural tracks, providing a rich blend of natural beauty.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Traiguera area?
A: Yes, many routes in Traiguera are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938350612/via-augusta-and-the-millenary-olive-trees-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Augusta and the Millenary Olive Trees Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 18.3-mile (29.4 km) circular route. Another option is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17389410" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Mateu – Santa Magdalena de Pulpis loop from Traiguera&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 52.5 miles (84.5 km).

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can visit along the cycling routes?
A: You can discover several interesting landmarks and natural features. Routes often pass by ancient olive groves, particularly on the slopes of the Sierra de Sant Pere. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5620781" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Sanctuary of Our Lady of the Fountain of Health of Traiguera&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3951882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Millennial Olive Tree of the Four Legs (Canet lo Roig)&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/662867" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cervera del Maestre Castle Ruins&lt;/a&gt; also offer a historical point of interest.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Traiguera offers several easy touring cycling routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33881504" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Moros&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.5-mile (7.2 km) route with minimal elevation gain, making it suitable for a relaxed ride. The region's network includes 9 easy routes, perfect for those seeking a less strenuous experience or cycling with family.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Traiguera?
A: The region is generally pleasant for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer mild temperatures, ideal for longer rides. Even in winter, the climate can be suitable, especially for enjoying the Mediterranean sun. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during those months.

Q: Where can I find parking facilities if I bring my own bike?
A: While specific parking facilities for cyclists at every trailhead are not detailed, Traiguera is well-equipped for cycling, especially with its 'RED BTT 100K of Traiguera' network. Generally, you can find parking in or near the main town of Traiguera, and many rural starting points for routes will have roadside parking available. It's advisable to check local signage.

Q: What is the difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Traiguera?
A: The touring cycling routes in Traiguera range from easy to difficult. There are 9 easy routes, 71 moderate routes, and 49 difficult routes. Difficult routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40342733" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sénia – Ulldecona Reservoir loop from Traiguera&lt;/a&gt;, can involve significant elevation gains and longer distances, while easy routes offer gentle gradients.

Q: Can I find places to eat or drink near the cycling trails in Traiguera?
A: Traiguera and its surrounding villages offer various cafes and restaurants where cyclists can refuel. The region is known for blending sport with local gastronomy, so you'll find opportunities to enjoy local cuisine. It's a good idea to plan your route to pass through towns or villages for convenient stops.

Q: Are there any accommodations suitable for cyclists in Traiguera?
A: Yes, Traiguera is a cycling-friendly destination. You can find accommodations like Casa de Olivos, which caters to cyclists, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6416466" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciudad del Ciclismo Campsite&lt;/a&gt;, specifically designed for cycling enthusiasts. Many local establishments are accustomed to hosting visitors exploring the area by bike.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Mediterranean Sea?
A: Yes, some touring cycling routes in Traiguera provide excellent views of the Mediterranean Sea, particularly from higher elevations or routes that venture closer to the coast. The region research indicates that cyclists can witness sunrises over the Mediterranean from various routes, and the nearby Sierra de Irta Natural Park features dramatic cliffs that descend to the sea.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about cycling in Traiguera?
A: The touring cycling experience in Traiguera is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 80 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning mountain and Mediterranean views, and the picturesque ancient olive groves. The well-developed network of routes, including the IMBA-approved 'RED BTT 100K of Traiguera,' is also a significant draw.

Q: Are there specific routes that explore the ancient olive groves?
A: Absolutely. The region is characterized by fields abundant with ancient olive trees, especially on the slopes of the Sierra de Sant Pere. A prime example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938350612/via-augusta-and-the-millenary-olive-trees-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Augusta and the Millenary Olive Trees Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which specifically highlights these scenic and historical groves.
tips:
Canet lo Roig has the largest number of ancient olive trees within the entire Valencian Community (1131 specimens).
Some of them date their planting to the year 569 AD.
Among so many specimens, the impressive "4 legs" stands out, which has a rich history, particularly related to the war serving as a refuge.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Via Augusta and the Millenary Olive Trees Loop?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily centers around the Valencian Community in Spain, particularly near Canet lo Roig in the Sénia Territory. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, you can typically find parking in or near the traditional villages along the route, such as Canet lo Roig, which serves as a good starting point for exploring the millenary olive groves.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not specify official rules regarding dogs on the cycling route. When cycling through rural areas and villages, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash and be mindful of local regulations and agricultural lands. Always ensure your dog is comfortable with the distance and terrain.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses diverse landscapes in the Valencian Community. To avoid the intense heat of summer and potential winter chill, spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) are generally the most pleasant times to cycle, offering mild temperatures and beautiful scenery.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are mentioned for cycling the Via Augusta and the Millenary Olive Trees Loop. The route largely follows public roads and paths through natural and agricultural areas.

faqs.question: What kind of bike is best suited for this route?
faqs.answer: While sections of the route follow the historical Via Augusta on paved or quiet secondary roads, there are also unpaved segments with loose gravel. A touring bicycle is suitable, but be prepared for occasional challenging sections where you might need to dismount and push your bike. Bikes with wider tires or a gravel bike would offer more comfort and stability on varied terrain.

faqs.question: Is the route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The route is generally categorized as a moderate bike ride. While the elevation gain is around 310 meters over 29.4 kilometers, some unpaved sections with gravel might be challenging for beginners. Those with good fitness and some experience with varied terrain will find it more enjoyable.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The primary draw of this route is the unique combination of historical significance and extraordinary natural heritage. You'll cycle through regions boasting the world's largest concentration of ancient olive trees, including the famous Millenary olive tree "4 patas". You'll also encounter sections of the ancient Roman Via Augusta, archaeological remains, Roman arches, and traditional villages that preserve a deep Roman legacy. The Sénia Territory features open-air museums and marked routes dedicated to these monumental trees.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while cycling?
faqs.answer: You can expect diverse scenery, including agricultural plains, gentle hills, and coastal areas if you extend beyond the loop. The highlight is cycling through groves of deeply gnarled, ancient olive trees, some over a thousand years old. Beyond these, you'll see rural roads passing through orange and almond groves, vineyards, and traditional Spanish villages.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Via Augusta de la Comunitat Valenciana, Camí Vell de Tortosa, and smaller local paths like Carrer Monxetades, Carrer del Portalet, Carrer de l'Escola, and Plaça d'Espanya.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes or accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, cycling through traditional villages like Canet lo Roig means you'll find opportunities to explore local gastronomy, visit oil mills, and experience authentic Spanish rural life. These villages typically offer cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain, including unpaved sections, it's advisable to pack essentials for a touring bicycle ride. This includes a repair kit, plenty of water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and appropriate cycling attire. Consider bringing a map or GPS device, as some sections might be less clearly marked. If you plan to dismount for gravel sections, comfortable shoes are also a good idea.
